hypothesis: If distorted crystal structures are optimized by gradient descent to minimize differentiable XRD pattern-similarity losses (cosine, MSE, or entropy), then they will rarely recover the ground-truth structures—even under correct symmetry constraints and moderate lattice/coordinate noise—because the XRD loss landscape over lattice and atomic coordinates is intrinsically rough with many local minima, leading to lower match rates than energy-based relaxation with CHGNet.
